methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vast is a late-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Jed McCaleb that has raised $300M+ to date. The broader sector comparison set includes 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies including SpaceX and Sierra Space, though a tight peer cohort at identical stage and valuation tier remains limited within the sector.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression anchoring. Using the $300M total raised as a proxy baseline, application of a standard 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Late Stage advancement yields $360M. Public Space & Aerospace comparables trade at a median P/S multiple of 25.8x across 6 tickers, but this approach cannot be applied without Vast's revenue disclosure. A narrow peer-median method also cannot be applied—while 11 same-sector peers exist in the dataset, none yielded sufficient datapoints (≥3 required) after filtering for stage and valuation tier alignment.

The triangulated range is $306M to $414M, with a median of $360M. This narrow distribution reflects methodological constraints rather than precision; the analysis is anchored to a single approach. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (needed for public-comp anchoring), unit economics, and near-term cash burn relative to the $300M+ raised. Absent additional financial disclosure, the $360M median represents the most defensible point estimate, with the $306M–$414M band capturing reasonable downside and upside scenarios.
